Fig. 3 Illustration of parameter inference and missing term discovery in PBPK modeling.

Fig. 1 Overall framework of MI-PINN. In Stage I, MI-PINN employs a multi-branch meta-representation learning scheme to learn the representation parameters θ across different training task configurations ξ to derive θ*. The final layer parameters ω* are computed via a physics-informed closed-form solution at each optimization step. In Stage II, the learned representation θ* is fixed, while the task-specific unknowns α are optimized for inverse modeling to derive α*. The final layer parameters are consistently computed using the same physicsinformed closed-form solution, with α replacing ξ.
